{{Infobox location
| name = Candle Bay Imperial Prison
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Candle Bay]] [[Imperial]] Prison, also referred to as the [[Candle Bay]] [[Imperial]] Prison and Reformative Facility, is a high-status correctional institution. The facility has been described as a house of torture and was eventually destroyed when an [[Elderspawn]] tore it open. This destruction resulted in significant casualties among the staff, including guards, alchemists, and clerical workers, while also allowing numerous dangerous inmates—such as murderers, black alchemists, and [[Soulbound]]—to escape into the [[Capital]]. Following this event, the prison was reported to have reopened. The facility's reception area is characterized by a cramped, uncomfortable design featuring small, unstable stools and white decor. Inmates within the prison are identified by bright red one-piece uniforms. While some accounts describe the prison as a place of hardship, other mentions of subterranean or [[island]]-based detention suggest certain cells could be relatively comfortable, providing amenities like blankets and books. The prison's history is linked to the [[Gray Island]], which contains a massive underground [[labyrinth]] of tunnels and cells originally established over a millennium ago by [[Jorin]] the Maze-walker.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Candle Bay]] [[Imperial]] Prison serves as the holding facility for [[Calder Marten's father]]. Although the prison has previously turned [[Calder]] away, he eventually finds himself positioned on the rocks beneath the structure. Inside, the facility features a small and cramped reception room decorated almost entirely in white. After [[Jerri]] enters the prison, the site is violently torn open by the [[Elderspawn]]. In the aftermath of this destruction, [[Calder Marten]] successfully breaks his father out of the prison.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]]'s father is locked in [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] is an [[Imperial]] Prison.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c10">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] is a location that has turned [[Calder]] away.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c13">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The reception room of [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] is small, cramped, and decorated almost entirely in white.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c15">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Jerri]] enters the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] found himself on the rocks beneath [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] was torn open by the [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder Marten]] broke his father out of the [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] is a location where [[Fletcher]] served as warden.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c2">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] is in ruins.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Kings and Killers ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Candle Bay Imperial Prison]] has just reopened.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c1">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“Candle Bay Imperial Prison has just reopened.”<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c1" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
